title: Deep red Banarasi saree for the pheras
concept_summary: A traditional pheras concept in a richly woven red Banarasi saree with a heavy zari border and a pleated pallu over the shoulder, worn over a fitted blouse with kundan detailing.

garment_breakdown.overall_form: A draped nine-yard-style saree over a fitted blouse, with the pallu carried over the shoulder.
garment_breakdown.silhouette: A body-skimming pleated drape that falls straight before the pallu sweeps over the shoulder.
garment_breakdown.drape_or_layering: Classic pleats at the waist with the pallu pinned and carried over the left shoulder.
garment_breakdown.key_proportions: The broad woven border anchors the hem while the pallu frames the upper body.

colour_story.palette_summary: A deep ceremonial red carried by lustrous gold zari.
colour_story.placement: Red fills the body of the saree while gold defines the border and pallu.
colour_story.rationale: Red and gold read as classic bridal ceremony colours for the pheras.

fabrics_and_texture.finish_and_movement: A weighty woven silk with a rich sheen and a structured, sculptural drape.

embellishment_plan.density: A richly worked surface with dense woven zari across the border and pallu.
embellishment_plan.restraint_notes: The dense work is anchored to the border and pallu so the drape still reads clearly.

coverage_and_drape.back_and_midriff: A traditional drape leaves a natural midriff between blouse and saree.
coverage_and_drape.dupatta_or_saree_drape: A pleated saree drape with the pallu carried over the left shoulder.

interpretation_notes: No broad regional direction was requested, so styling stays general.
safeguards: The saree is kept as a draped garment, not restyled as a stitched gown.

styling_notes: Pair with classic temple jewellery to suit the ceremony.
construction_caveats: This is a concept visualisation and is not a sewing pattern.
It does not guarantee that the garment can be constructed exactly as shown.
image_alt_text: A model in a red Banarasi saree with a gold zari border and a pleated pallu over the shoulder for the pheras.
